---
title: "Core Concepts in Practical Program Evaluation"
description: "Synthesis note on recurring concepts in Practical Program Evaluation."
book_id: "practical-program-evaluation"
collection_id: "evaluation-texts"
note_type: "Synthesis"
citation_key: "chen_2015_practical_program_evaluation"
evidence_ids:
  - "chunk:practical-program-evaluation:ch01-evaluation-design-and-its-components:c0002"
  - "chunk:practical-program-evaluation:ch03-logic-models-and-the-action-model-change-model-schema-program-theory:c0001"
  - "chunk:practical-program-evaluation:ch13-a-guide-to-productively-applying-the-logic-model-and-the-action-model-change-mod:c0001"
source_status: "needs_review"
---
## Source

This synthesis draws across the generated notes for *Practical Program Evaluation* [@chen_2015_practical_program_evaluation].

## Central Logic

Evaluation design should match a program's stage, stakeholder needs, and theory of how action is expected to produce change.

## Concepts

- Evaluation design: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Program stage: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Logic model: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Action model/change model schema: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Stakeholder use: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.
- Constructive and conclusive evaluation: a recurring idea used to frame evaluation design, evidence collection, interpretation, or use in this book.

## Links

- [Evaluation Design and Its Components](section-01-evaluation-design-components.qmd)
- [Logic Models and the Action Model/Change Model Schema](section-02-logic-models-and-program-theory.qmd)
- [When Logic Models Do Not Work as Expected](section-03-logic-model-limits.qmd)

## References
